title: Obadiah 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Obadiah
paragraphs: 1 This is the vision of Obadiah. This is what the Lord GOD says about Edom. We have heard a message from the LORD. A messenger has been sent to the nations to say, "Get up, and let us go to fight against her!"
2 "Look, I will make you small among the nations. You will be hated very much.
3 The pride of your heart has tricked you. You live in cracks in the rocks. Your home is high up. You say in your heart, 'Who can bring me down?'
4 Even if you fly high like an eagle and make your nest among the stars, I will bring you down from there," says the LORD.
5 "If thieves came to you, if robbers came by night— oh, how ruined you will be!— would they not steal only what they wanted? If grape pickers came to you, would they not leave a few grapes behind?
6 But how Esau will be robbed! His hidden treasures will be found!
7 All your friends will push you to the border. The men at peace with you will trick and defeat you. Those who eat your bread will set a trap for you. You will not see it coming.
8 "On that day," says the LORD, "will I not destroy the wise men of Edom? Will I not destroy the wise men in the mountains of Esau?
9 Then your strong men, O Teman, will be very afraid. Every man in the mountains of Esau will be cut down and killed.
10 Because of the violence against your brother Jacob, shame will cover you. You will be cut off forever.
11 On the day you stood far away, strangers carried off his wealth. Foreigners entered his gate and threw lots for Jerusalem. On that day, you were just like one of them.

12 But you should not have been glad on that day. It was your brother's day of trouble. You should not have been happy over the people of Judah on the day of their destruction. You should not have boasted on the day of their distress.

13 You should not have entered the gate of My people on the day of their disaster. You should not have been glad about their trouble on the day of their disaster. You should not have taken their wealth on the day of their disaster.
14 You should not have stood at the crossroads to cut off those running away. You should not have handed over their survivors on the day of their distress.
15 For the Day of the LORD is near for all the nations. As you have done, it will be done to you. Your payback will return on your own head.
16 As you drank on My holy mountain, so all the nations will drink again and again. They will drink and swallow it down. They will be as if they had never lived.
17 But on Mount Zion there will be rescue. It will be holy. The house of Jacob will take back what is theirs.

18 Then the house of Jacob will be a burning fire. The house of Joseph will be a hot flame. But the house of Esau will be dry straw. Jacob will set it on fire and burn it up. So no one from the house of Esau will be left." For the LORD has spoken.

19 Those from the Negev will take the mountains of Esau. Those from the foothills will take the land of the Philistines. They will live in the fields of Ephraim and Samaria. Benjamin will take Gilead.
20 The exiles of the Israelites will take the land of the Canaanites as far as Zarephath. The exiles from Jerusalem who are in Sepharad will take the cities of the Negev.
21 The rescuers will go up Mount Zion to rule over the mountains of Esau. And the kingdom will belong to the LORD.
